label: battery
description: The anode material is a core component in lithium-ion batteries that stores lithium ions and releases them during discharge to generate electricity, playing a critical role in determining the charging speed, lifespan, and structural stability of the battery. The basic structure of the anode material typically consists of a coating of a mixture of anode active material, conductive agents, and binders on a copper substrate. Related patents cover various technologies for the manufacturing and performance improvement of anode materials, including methods for producing anode active materials by mixing lithium compounds with nickel, manganese, and cobalt compounds, as well as technologies for extracting lithium from liquid resources through ion exchange reactors. In particular, surface modification of anode materials, optimization of particle size, and precursor manufacturing processes at high temperatures appear as significant technical applications aimed at enhancing the performance of lithium-ion batteries. These technologies contribute to increasing the energy density and stability of lithium-ion batteries and improving the charging and discharging efficiency. However, there may be specific chemical components or processes excluded regarding anode materials, such as certain environmentally friendly materials or non-traditional manufacturing methods that may not be included.
example_papers: Electrochemical cell, e.g., solid-state battery, comprises solid-state electrolyte, anode, and interfacial layer between the solid-state electrolyte and the anode, where interfacial layer comprises lithium metal sulfide

Lithium-metal negative electrode used in lithium-metal liquid-electrolyte electrochemical cell, comprises negative-electrode base layer comprising base-layer metal including aluminum and titanium, protection layer arranged on and supported by negative-electrode base layer

Silicon nanostructure-based anode used for lithium-ion battery for e.g. portable electronics, toys and cars, consists of composite of amorphous polysilicon or crystalline silicon-containing films having preset thickness

Lithium cell used for lithium metal battery, comprises electrolyte material, cathode structure arranged on one side of electrolyte material, cathode structure including cathode electrode and cathode current collector, and anode structure arranged on opposite side of electrolyte material

Making anode for electrochemical energy storage device, involves mixing silicon particles and at least one polymer to form mixture, coating mixture onto copper current collector to form coated copper current collector and subjecting coated copper current collector to temperature treatment

Preparing capillary-suspension based graphite anode for making graphite anodes for lithium-ion batteries using capillary-suspension-based ink slurry, involves dissolving aqueous binder in water to form a gel, suspending conductive additive and active material in gel

Energy storage device electrode, comprises silicon substrate with one or more trenches, each of trenches has trench base with electrically active surface with electrically insulated sidewalk and field regions, active silicon surface
High energy anode used in electronic devices, comprises active core and porous protective shell having high specific capacity with stable charge and discharge cycle performance

Nanocomposite used in energy storage device, comprises set of dispersed nanoparticles comprising core and shell and are configured to absorb and release lithium ions, and set of electrically coupled carbon particles

Composition of matter used as electrode in electrochemical cell e.g., electrochemical cell, fuel cell, or lithium-ion battery, comprises composite comprising reduced compound, where reduced compound comprises particles, particles comprises grain embedded in silicon dioxide, grain comprises silicon

children.label: graphite_anode_materials
children.description: Graphite-based anode materials are essential components of lithium-ion batteries, serving to efficiently store and release lithium ions in the form of LiC₆. This material contributes to improved battery performance by providing low cost, long lifespan, and high stability. Natural graphite (NG) exhibits relatively high capacity (350-370 mAh/g) but has lower output characteristics, whereas artificial graphite (AG) is produced by heat treating pitch and coke at high temperatures, offering fast charging and excellent lifespan (280-360 mAh/g) due to its high degree of crystallization. Artificial graphite optimizes the diffusion pathway of lithium ions by controlling particle size and pore structure, and it maintains high discharge efficiency even at low temperatures. Mixed anode materials of natural and artificial types are utilized as universal products that strike a balance between cost and performance, applicable in various electronic devices and electric vehicles. Patents in this field include technological innovations such as electrode structures, combinations of conductive materials, and optimization of electrolytes, presenting various approaches to maximizing the performance of lithium-ion batteries. These graphite-based anode materials are designed with a structure where a mixture of active anode material, conductive agents, and binders is coated onto a copper (Cu) substrate, which is a fundamental component of the electrode. The advancement of battery technology is exploring methods to simultaneously improve energy density and charging speed.

children.label: natural_graphite_anode_material
children.description: The anode material manufactured by refining and spheroidizing natural graphite is an essential component of lithium secondary batteries, serving to efficiently store and release lithium ions to generate electrical energy. This anode material boasts high crystallinity and graphitization, leading to excellent initial charge efficiency and long lifespan, along with being cost-effective and highly stable. However, the low output characteristics of natural graphite limit its performance in applications requiring fast charging or high power. Related patents address methods for manufacturing anode materials utilizing natural graphite, focusing on performance optimization techniques such as controlling particle size and adjusting mixing ratios during the refining process. In particular, the development of hybrid anode materials that combine natural and artificial graphite is making significant progress in balancing performance with cost. These technologies aim to optimize lithium ion diffusion pathways and maintain high discharge efficiency at low temperatures, providing electrode materials suitable for various battery applications. Therefore, natural graphite-based anode materials have the potential to play a crucial role in electric vehicles and other renewable energy storage systems. However, they do have limitations in their suitability for industrial applications with high power demands.

children.label: graphite_anode_materials
children.description: The anode materials made from petroleum-based coke and pitch through high-temperature pyrolysis are key components that maximize the performance of lithium-ion batteries, exhibiting excellent structural stability and rapid charging capabilities. Although these anode materials are relatively more expensive than natural graphite, they provide high output and long lifespan characteristics, making them suitable for electric vehicles and high-performance battery applications. Major technological applications include the heat treatment and crystallization processes of pitch and coke in synthetic graphite manufacturing, which contribute to optimizing the diffusion pathways of lithium ions and maintaining high discharge efficiency even at low temperatures. Relevant patents mention methods for producing mixtures of anode active materials for lithium-ion batteries, optimizing electrochemical reactions between the anode and cathode, and controlling the particle size and porosity structure of the anode materials. These technologies have the potential to improve the charging speed, lifespan, and electrical performance of lithium-ion batteries. For example, synthetic graphite has a capacity of 280–360 mAh/g, which ensures reliability even at lower capacities compared to natural graphite. Such petroleum-based coke and pitch anode materials can be utilized as versatile products that balance cost and performance in battery manufacturing. This classification emphasizes their differentiated performance in comparison to natural graphite and low-cost anode materials, focusing on high-temperature pyrolyzed anode materials and their applications.
example_papers: Preparing capillary-suspension based graphite anode for making graphite anodes for lithium-ion batteries using capillary-suspension-based ink slurry, involves dissolving aqueous binder in water to form a gel, suspending conductive additive and active material in gel

Battery comprises separator separating anode and cathode, and lithium electrolyte, where cathode comprising active powder plus binder, active powder does not have atomic layer deposition coating, and anode comprising coated graphite powder and binder

Negative electrode sheet used in secondary battery for electric device, comprises negative electrode current collector and negative electrode film layer positioned on one surface of negative electrode current collector

Preparation method for improving fast charging performance of graphite negative electrode material used for lithium-ion battery, by thermally kneading artificial graphite material with solid- and liquid-phase carbon-based coating agents, pressing into graphite sheet, carbonizing and depolymerizing

children.label: anode_material_graphite_mixture_general
children.description: A universal anode material that combines natural and artificial graphite or can apply both types is a key material that effectively stores and releases lithium ions during the charging and discharging processes of lithium secondary batteries, playing an important role in determining the battery's electrical performance, lifespan, and stability. This anode material balances cost and performance by combining the advantages of natural graphite (NG) and artificial graphite (AG), while providing flexibility for application in various battery designs. In particular, natural graphite offers cost-effectiveness and high capacity (350–370 mAh/g), while artificial graphite, produced through high-temperature heat treatment, boasts high crystallinity along with fast charging and high power performance (280–360 mAh/g). Relevant patent literature discusses technologies for optimizing the diffusion pathways of lithium ions through controlling the particle size and pore structure of graphite materials during the manufacturing process of the anode material, maintaining high discharge efficiency even at low temperatures. For example, the manufacturing method of graphite-based anode materials involves mixing various raw materials to form an anode active material with optimal electrical properties. These technologies increase the potential for use in various applications such as electric vehicles and portable electronic devices, contributing to the improvement of efficiency in energy storage systems. This taxonomy focuses on the application of anode materials in lithium secondary batteries, and may exclude specific application areas depending on the composition or physical properties of the raw materials.

children.label: silicon_anode_materials
children.description: Silicon (Si)-based anode materials are key components of lithium-ion batteries, playing a vital role in storing lithium ions that migrate from the cathode and generating electricity during discharge. This anode material offers up to 10 times the theoretical capacity compared to graphite, making it suitable for high-energy-density batteries. The main technological applications include SiOx, Si-C, and Pure Si types, and these materials employ techniques such as carbon matrix bonding, surface coating, and doping to address issues of volumetric expansion and low initial efficiency. For instance, high-capacity lithium-ion silicon-based composite materials are produced by mixing modified Si/C materials with a porous structure and the piezoelectric material PbLa0.04Zr0.52Ti0.48O3. Moreover, pure silicon anode materials are beneficial for solid-state batteries, incorporating self-healing adhesives and conductive polymer coatings. The design of this anode material acts as a critical factor determining the charging rate, lifespan, and structural stability of the battery, with certain patented technologies evolving to improve conductivity by utilizing advanced materials like graphene or maximizing efficiency through low-temperature synthesis. However, minimizing the environmental impact that may arise during this process will be a key challenge moving forward.
example_papers: Silicon nanostructure-based anode used for lithium-ion battery for e.g. portable electronics, toys and cars, consists of composite of amorphous polysilicon or crystalline silicon-containing films having preset thickness

Making anode for electrochemical energy storage device, involves mixing silicon particles and at least one polymer to form mixture, coating mixture onto copper current collector to form coated copper current collector and subjecting coated copper current collector to temperature treatment

Energy storage device electrode, comprises silicon substrate with one or more trenches, each of trenches has trench base with electrically active surface with electrically insulated sidewalk and field regions, active silicon surface
High energy anode used in electronic devices, comprises active core and porous protective shell having high specific capacity with stable charge and discharge cycle performance

Nanocomposite used in energy storage device, comprises set of dispersed nanoparticles comprising core and shell and are configured to absorb and release lithium ions, and set of electrically coupled carbon particles

Composition of matter used as electrode in electrochemical cell e.g., electrochemical cell, fuel cell, or lithium-ion battery, comprises composite comprising reduced compound, where reduced compound comprises particles, particles comprises grain embedded in silicon dioxide, grain comprises silicon

Battery electrode composition comprises population of composite particles, comprising silicon and carbon, population is particle size distribution as determined by laser particle size distribution analysis

Manufacture of electrode material for forming electrode, involves producing aerosol from stock containing active electrode material-containing particles and graphene components, and collecting composition containing particles on substrate
Porous carbon/silicon oxide composite used in anode for lithium battery, comprises porous carbon structure host having pores, and silicon oxide coating or particle residing in at least one of pores

Manufacture of material for anode, involves coating several core structures comprising silicon particles with sol-gel solution comprising vanadium oxide and thermal treating partially coated core structures and coating core-shell structures with sol-gel solution and thermal treating

children.label: silicon_anode_siox
children.description: The technology of using silicon oxide to form a protective layer of Li₂O·Li₄SiO₄ during charging, which suppresses volume expansion and enhances structural stability, focuses on the development of anode materials for high energy density lithium secondary batteries. This technology plays a crucial role in storing lithium ions in the anode and generating electricity during discharge, acting as an important factor that determines the charging speed, lifespan, and structural stability of the battery. Silicon (Si)-based anode materials offer up to 10 times the theoretical capacity compared to graphite and exist in forms such as SiOx, Si-C, and Pure Si, where techniques like carbon matrix incorporation, surface coating, and doping are applied to address volume expansion and initial efficiency issues. Related patents present innovative methods to enhance the efficiency of low-conductivity silicon anode materials, including methods for lithium extraction, solid electrolytes, and the manufacture of active anode materials. For instance, there are methods for producing lithium metal anodes or lithium-silicon alloy/carbon composites, which contribute to enhancing the performance of lithium-ion batteries. While this technology can aid in the commercialization of high energy density batteries, there are limitations due to low conductivity characteristics that may reduce the overall performance of the batteries. Therefore, continuous research is necessary for the commercialization of lithium-ion batteries.
example_papers: High energy anode used in electronic devices, comprises active core and porous protective shell having high specific capacity with stable charge and discharge cycle performance

Composition of matter used as electrode in electrochemical cell e.g., electrochemical cell, fuel cell, or lithium-ion battery, comprises composite comprising reduced compound, where reduced compound comprises particles, particles comprises grain embedded in silicon dioxide, grain comprises silicon
Porous carbon/silicon oxide composite used in anode for lithium battery, comprises porous carbon structure host having pores, and silicon oxide coating or particle residing in at least one of pores

children.label: si_c_anode_material
children.description: Si-C anode materials are innovative battery anode materials composed of nano-sized silicon combined with a carbon matrix, aiming to secure the high-capacity characteristics of silicon (1,400–1,800 mAh/g) alongside the electrical conductivity and mechanical stability of carbon. This structure suppresses the volume expansion of silicon, improving cycle life and contributing to maximizing the performance of anode materials in lithium secondary batteries. Major technological applications include manufacturing methods utilizing chemical vapor deposition (CVD) or mechanical mixing techniques, focusing on improving the conductivity of silicon or addressing initial efficiency issues. Related patents encompass methods of combining with the carbon matrix, surface coating technologies, and doping technologies, all contributing to optimizing the performance of various silicon-based anode materials. For example, the composite structure of silicon and carbon provides high electrical conductivity and strong mechanical properties, ensuring fast charging rates and prolonged cycle life. Due to these characteristics, Si-C anode materials have established themselves as key materials for high-energy-density batteries, enhancing both the stability and efficiency of cells. However, Si-C anode materials also have limitations, such as high manufacturing costs and uniformity issues during mass production.
example_papers: Silicon nanostructure-based anode used for lithium-ion battery for e.g. portable electronics, toys and cars, consists of composite of amorphous polysilicon or crystalline silicon-containing films having preset thickness
High energy anode used in electronic devices, comprises active core and porous protective shell having high specific capacity with stable charge and discharge cycle performance

Nanocomposite used in energy storage device, comprises set of dispersed nanoparticles comprising core and shell and are configured to absorb and release lithium ions, and set of electrically coupled carbon particles

Battery electrode composition comprises population of composite particles, comprising silicon and carbon, population is particle size distribution as determined by laser particle size distribution analysis

Manufacture of electrode material for forming electrode, involves producing aerosol from stock containing active electrode material-containing particles and graphene components, and collecting composition containing particles on substrate

Manufacture of material for anode, involves coating several core structures comprising silicon particles with sol-gel solution comprising vanadium oxide and thermal treating partially coated core structures and coating core-shell structures with sol-gel solution and thermal treating

Production of silicon-carbon composite used as negative electrode active material in all-solid-state battery, involves supplying silicon particles into reactor, forming inorganic layer on silicon particles, forming carbon film on obtained composite, and removing portion of inorganic layer
Preparation of silicon-carbon composite material used for electrode sheet, involves using one-step method to calcine mixture comprising silicon dioxide source, magnesium powder and carbon source

Preparation of silicon-carbon composite material used for e.g. electrode, involves carbonizing and reducing mixture comprising carbon matrix and composite template agent to obtain porous carbon having preset specific surface area and performing silane deposition and gas-phase carbon coating

Silicon-carbon negative electrode material for electrode for battery, comprises nitrogen-doped porous graphene matrix, nitrogen-doped nano-silicon particles which are dispersedly distributed inside and/or on surface of nitrogen-doped porous graphene matrix, and carbon coating layer

children.label: anode_material_si_based_si_c_crushed_type
children.description: The technology of mixing and grinding nano-silicon with carbon materials, wherein the carbon matrix mechanically suppresses volume expansion, is a key approach in the development of anode materials for lithium secondary batteries. The main objective of this technology is to utilize the high theoretical capacity of silicon (1,400–1,800 mAh/g) while addressing the issue of silicon's volume expansion, thereby enhancing initial efficiency and structural stability. Relevant patents primarily include technical approaches aimed at improving charge rates and cycle life through composite structures that combine nanoscale silicon with a carbon matrix. These anode materials are manufactured using chemical vapor deposition (CVD) methods or grinding and mixing techniques, maximizing the high-capacity characteristics of silicon through the electrical conductivity and mechanical stability of carbon. Related technologies involve methods for producing anode active materials, processes for lithium extraction, and adjustments of the structural characteristics of electrode materials. Moreover, due to the limited mixing ratio of silicon and carbon, research is continuously underway to find the optimal combination. In this context, nano-silicon-carbon composites are contributing to the development of high-energy-density batteries and play a significant role in enhancing the performance of next-generation lithium secondary batteries.

children.label: silicon_based_anode_materials
children.description: The technology of depositing silicon onto a carbon substrate to form a uniform Si layer is an important methodology for maximizing the performance of lithium secondary battery anode materials. This technology optimizes the electrochemical reaction area that occurs when lithium ions move from the anode to the cathode, greatly improving the battery's charging speed and lifespan. By primarily utilizing a Si-C composite structure, the focus is on simultaneously securing the high theoretical capacity of silicon (1,400-1,800 mAh/g) and the electrical conductivity and mechanical stability of carbon.

children.label: si_based_anode_material_pure_si
children.description: High-purity silicon or alloys with a Si content of over 50 wt% serve as an anode material for lithium secondary batteries, possessing a very high theoretical capacity, making them suitable for high energy density batteries. However, these materials require structural stabilization technologies due to issues such as volume expansion and initial efficiency degradation during charge and discharge processes. Related technologies include carbon matrix bonding, surface coating, and doping, all of which play a crucial role in mitigating volume changes of silicon and improving initial efficiency. For instance, Si-C composites maximize the theoretical capacity of silicon while enhancing mechanical strength to improve the structural stability of the electrodes. Additionally, specific surface coating techniques enhance corrosion resistance and conductivity, thus improving the overall performance of the battery. Such technologies maximize the performance of lithium-ion batteries and act as critical factors determining the battery's lifespan and charging rate. This classification focuses on materials with a Si content of over 50 wt% and excludes alloys or alternative anode materials with lower Si content.
example_papers: Making anode for electrochemical energy storage device, involves mixing silicon particles and at least one polymer to form mixture, coating mixture onto copper current collector to form coated copper current collector and subjecting coated copper current collector to temperature treatment

Energy storage device electrode, comprises silicon substrate with one or more trenches, each of trenches has trench base with electrically active surface with electrically insulated sidewalk and field regions, active silicon surface

Negative electrode active material used in secondary batteries as main power source for e.g. mobile communication devices, comprises silicate composite particles containing silicate phase dispersed in silicate phase, and silicon phase containing element phosphorus in addition to silicon
Composite particle used in electrode mixture for electrode layer of battery, comprises active material containing silicon element or tin element, and binder

children.label: anode_material_li_metal_system
children.description: The next-generation materials that utilize lithium metal directly as an anode achieve the highest energy density (3,860 mAh/g) and are key elements driving the innovation of various battery technologies, such as solid-state batteries (SSB) and lithium-sulfur (Li-S) batteries. This technology maximizes battery performance through the efficient storage and release of lithium ions, and includes advancements in anode materials that determine battery charge speed, lifespan, and structural stability. Major technical challenges include dendrite suppression, interfacial stabilization, and the formation of protective layers, which contribute to improving the cycle stability of lithium metal anodes. Related patents address the fabrication of solid electrolytes, the composition of ion conductors, and the structural optimization of electrodes, particularly focusing on solid electrolytes that enhance the overall performance of lithium batteries by incorporating ionic conductivity and electronic conductivity composites. For instance, the performance of lithium-sulfur batteries can be maximized through the coating and structural improvement of electrode active materials. This technology also encompasses electrolyte compositions that maintain stability in both high and low-temperature environments, which will be essential for the battery technologies of next-generation electric vehicles and mobile devices. Therefore, the development of anode materials that directly use lithium metal will lead to innovation in next-generation battery technologies and play a crucial role in providing sustainable energy solutions.
example_papers: Electrochemical cell, e.g., solid-state battery, comprises solid-state electrolyte, anode, and interfacial layer between the solid-state electrolyte and the anode, where interfacial layer comprises lithium metal sulfide

Lithium-metal negative electrode used in lithium-metal liquid-electrolyte electrochemical cell, comprises negative-electrode base layer comprising base-layer metal including aluminum and titanium, protection layer arranged on and supported by negative-electrode base layer

Lithium cell used for lithium metal battery, comprises electrolyte material, cathode structure arranged on one side of electrolyte material, cathode structure including cathode electrode and cathode current collector, and anode structure arranged on opposite side of electrolyte material

Energy storage device electrode, comprises silicon substrate with one or more trenches, each of trenches has trench base with electrically active surface with electrically insulated sidewalk and field regions, active silicon surface

Material used in anode of lithium battery cell, comprises lithium metal foil having lithium foil thickness and comprising lithium metal and crystalline defects containing lithium and other element chosen from hydrogen, oxygen and nitrogen

Lithium-carbon composite material used in negative electrode sheet for secondary battery, comprises lithium metal core, protective layer coated on surface of lithium metal core, and porous carbon layer coated on surface of protective layer

children.label: lithium_metal_anode
children.description: The technology of directly using lithium metal as the anode is a key approach to achieving the highest energy density (3,860 mAh/g) in lithium secondary batteries. This technology aims to significantly enhance the overall performance of the battery by utilizing lithium metal as an electrode material. However, the use of lithium metal anodes is accompanied by several challenges due to dendrite formation and interfacial side reactions. To address these issues, protective layers and lithium preintercalation techniques are essential. Related patents propose various methods for forming protective layers to suppress dendrite growth and techniques for stabilizing interfaces, such as combining lithium metal anodes with polymers to enhance stability. Additionally, various application technologies, such as manufacturing methods for active materials in lithium-ion batteries, are being developed. These technologies are expected to maximize the performance of lithium metal electrodes and be applicable to next-generation battery systems such as solid-state batteries (SSB) and lithium-sulfur (Li-S) batteries. Such innovations are anticipated to contribute to the commercialization of lithium metal batteries and the advancement of energy storage technologies.

children.label: lithium_metal_anode_technology
children.description: The structure in which lithium precipitates without an anode active material during the initial manufacturing process of lithium secondary batteries offers significant advantages in energy density and weight reduction. The primary goal of this structure is to achieve an energy density of up to 3,860 mAh/g by directly using lithium metal as the anode. However, securing the re-deposition efficiency of lithium and the lifespan of the battery remains a key technical challenge. Related patent technologies address issues such as dendrite suppression, interface stabilization, and protective layer formation that may occur during the movement and storage of lithium ions. For instance, when directly using lithium without an anode active material, various process technologies are needed to ensure the structural stability of the electrode. Additionally, patents related to the extraction from lithium salts and electrode manufacturing propose efficient ion exchange and impurity removal methods for lithium, incorporating various methodologies to enhance battery performance. These technologies focus on applications such as optimizing anode material performance, increasing charging speed, and extending lifespan. However, this taxonomy excludes technologies that do not directly use lithium metal or deal with traditional anode material components of lithium-ion batteries.

children.label: li_metal_anodes
children.description: Li-Metal is used as an anode material for lithium secondary batteries and is primarily extracted through electrolysis from raw materials such as LiCl, LiOH, and Li₂CO₃ using a molten salt electrolysis method. In this process, a eutectic system with KCl is utilized to lower the melting temperature, and subsequent processes including low-temperature vacuum distillation and purification, deep gas purification, and slag treatment achieve a high purity of ≥99.9%. This technology is becoming established as a next-generation material where lithium metal is directly used as an anode, including process improvements for continuous production and energy savings. Major technical applications include the use of lithium-sulfur (Li-S) batteries and solid-state batteries (SSB), where dendrite suppression, interface stabilization, and protective layer formation emerge as key technological challenges. Related patents address innovative approaches to lithium extraction and purification processes, methods for manufacturing anode active materials, and surface modification of lithium metal anodes. For example, the lithium metal anode incorporates a polymer formed by surface polymerization of the lithium layer, enhancing electrochemical stability. These technologies contribute to improving the charging rate, lifespan, and structural stability of batteries, evolving towards maximizing the performance of next-generation lithium secondary batteries. This taxonomy focuses on the development of lithium metal anodes and related technologies, concentrating on research into new materials and processes related to the anode materials of lithium-ion batteries. Excluded are general cathode materials of lithium-ion batteries or an overall technical description of lithium-ion batteries.

children.label: li_metal_anode_manufacturing
children.description: The manufacturing of lithium metal involves the use of electrolysis cells, electrodes, collection, storage, and transportation devices, as well as gas treatment and purification systems to produce high-performance anode materials for lithium secondary batteries. The core of this process is to directly use lithium metal as an anode to maximize energy density and improve the charging speed, lifespan, and structural stability of the battery. Major technological applications include multi-graphite anodes, sealed humidity-controlled electrolysis cells, vacuum distillation units, and slag treatment devices, which enhance production efficiency, current efficiency, and purity while ensuring impurity removal and safety. Related patents address the formation of protective layers for lithium metal anodes, dendrite suppression technologies, and methods to improve interactions with electrolytes. For instance, the impact of temperature and humidity control technologies in electrolysis cells on electrode performance is discussed, along with the deformation and synthesis methods of lithium metal anode materials. These technologies are also applicable to next-generation battery systems such as lithium-sulfur batteries and solid-state batteries (SSB), driving innovations in modern energy storage devices. The manufacturing of lithium metal anodes plays a crucial role in maximizing the performance of lithium-ion batteries and ensuring the safety and stability of the cells. This description covers applications and technical details related to lithium metal batteries but does not include descriptions of specific electrolysis cell systems or anode material combinations.
